content: The Verge The Verge is about technology and how it makes us feel. Founded in 2011, we offer our audience everything from breaking news to reviews to award-winning features and investigations, on our site, in video, and in podcasts. 2026-07-24T15:14:04+00:00 https://www.theverge.com/rss/index.xml https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/01/verge-rss-large_80b47e.png?w=150&h=150&crop=1 Andrew J. Hawkins https://www.theverge.com/?p=970560 2026-07-24T11:14:04-04:00 2026-07-24T11:14:04-04:00 A door handle on a Tesla Model Y at a charging station in San Francisco, California, US, on Monday, Nov. 10, 2025. | David Paul Morris/Bloomberg via Getty Images Tesla's electronic door handles that have been linked to several deaths could lead to tougher safety rules for the entire auto industry. In a notice published today , the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration said that complaints about Tesla's mechanical door release don't warrant a defect investigation. Instead, the issue is better addressed through a broader rulemaking that would "mandate a robust and obvious door egress system in all motor vehicles," the safety regulator said. If finalized, all new vehicles would be subject to the new requirements. Tesla's door handles, which operate electronically and sit flush against the ve … Read the full story at The Verge. ]]> Cameron Faulkner https://www.theverge.com/?p=970559 2026-07-24T11:11:03-04:00 2026-07-24T11:11:03-04:00 The Spin 514 has a 16:10 aspect ratio IPS touchscreen with a 120Hz refresh rate — impressive specs for a sub-$600 Chromebook. | Image: The Verge Most Chromebooks ship with only 8GB of RAM, which is enough for some people, but certainly not enough for me. Multitaskers who want more memory in their next Chromebook should check out Best Buy , which is discounting Acer’s Chromebook Plus Spin 514 for the remainder of the day. Originally $749 for the configuration with 12GB of RAM, a MediaTek Kompanio Ultra 910 processor, and 256GB of UFS storage, it’s down to $559 right now. It’s a good price for what we consider to be one of the best Chromebooks you can currently buy. Acer Chromebook Plus Spin 514 (2025) Where to Buy: $749 $5599 at Best Buy My colleague Antonio G. Di Benedetto had a lot of nice things to say about this model in his October 2025 review. He praised the capabilities of its Arm-based processor, which is uncommon for a Chromebook review, as well as its day-plus battery life. The Spin 514 is great to use as a laptop, with a tactile keyboard and satisfying mechanical trackpad, but its 14-inch 1920 x 1200 120Hz IPS touchscreen can bend backwards to be used as a tablet. No stylus is included, but you can use any USI 2.0 stylus you’d like. If you want to know about other good discounted options, Lenovo’s Chromebook Plus 14 that Antonio likes a bit more is $649 (originally $999) at Best Buy . While it has the same processor and storage amount, it’s the model to buy instead if you want a little more RAM (16GB), an OLED screen, superior speakers, and fingerprint biometric support. Read our review of the Acer Chromebook Plus Spin 514. window.HYPE_DESK_CONFIG = { productImageUrl: "https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/d1cc9c21-89fe-4a6c-b440-68fb6993e28f.avif", productImageAlt: "Lenovo ThinkPad T14", productTitle: "Lenovo ThinkPad T14", shortDescription: "The reliable business laptop, available refurbished at a fraction of the price.", longDescription: "The ThinkPad T14 has been the go-to business laptop for a reason. Built for the demands of hybrid and remote work with processors that handle video conferencing, spreadsheets, coding, and presentations without slowing down. AI acceleration makes everyday productivity tasks faster and the build quality is exactly what you expect from a ThinkPad. Available refurbished on Back Market for significantly less than buying new.", purchaseUrl: "https://www.awin1.com/cread.php?awinmid=18275&awinaffid=173843&campaign=VergeHypeDesk&clickref=Article4&platform=dl&ued=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.backmarket.com%2Fen-us%2Fp%2Flenovo-thinkpad-t14-g1-14-inch-2020-core-i5-10310u-16-gb-ssd-256-gb%2Fb898556f-a07a-46c9-b70c-ad6133a7f2c8%3Fl%3D12", purchaseLinkText: "Shop At Back Market" }; Other great, Verge-approved deals Anker’s 140W wall charger with three USB-C ports and a USB-A port is selling at an especially low price at eBay via Best Buy’s seller page . The charger costs $55.99 (originally $79.99). Meanwhile, it’s $79.99 for Prime members at Amazon , but includes a USB-C cable whereas the Best Buy option doesn’t. One of the most notable traits of this charger — its downward facing ports that reduce its sag in the outlet — has become common since it launched early last year. The silver charger can deliver up to 140W through two of its USB-C ports when a single device is plugged in. It automatically splits the power depending on how many gadgets you have plugged in. If you’re a Sam’s Club member , or perhaps may soon become one, you can get $100 in digital Nintendo gift cards for $79.98. You can purchase up to five, which is enough to buy a bunch of games and accessories through Nintendo, or even a console. It’ll cost you $400 to get $500 worth of credit, meaning you can save on a Switch 2 console if you want to stack the savings to their maximum. The Switch 2 will increase in price starting in September , rising from $449.99 to $499.99, so if you’re on the fence, buying one sooner than later is a wise choice. Many of Logitech’s recent PC accessories can connect wirelessly via 2.4GHz to its Bolt receiver. Whether you lost your old one, or perhaps you want to upgrade to the newer USB-C version of the Bolt receiver that’s included with the MX Master 4 mouse, Amazon and Walmart have USB-C Bolt receivers available at just over half off. Originally $14.99, they’re $7.24, making it less expensive to grab a spare to connect your devices to. If you want to pair your device to a new Bolt receiver, you’ll need to do that through Logitech’s Options Plus app on desktop PC. ]]> Stevie Bonifield https://www.theverge.com/?p=970618 2026-07-24T11:00:03-04:00 2026-07-24T11:00:03-04:00 Boox's new Tile lineup will take on Xteink's popular e-readers that are small enough to stick on the back of an iPhone with MagSafe. As spotted by Good E-Reader , the first one is the Picco, which will feature a 3.97-inch front-lit, black-and-white e-paper display and expandable storage with an SD card slot. Boox confirmed to The Verge that the Picco is in the works, noting that it's part of a new Tile lineup and will have an "accessible price point," but without any other details about its plans, like specs, the specific price, or a release date. The front-lit display could put it on par with Xteink's new $99 X4 Pro e-reader , which The Ver … Read the full story at The Verge. ]]> Robert Hart https://www.theverge.com/?p=970534 2026-07-24T10:44:15-04:00 2026-07-24T10:43:55-04:00 Trump’s science adviser Michael Kratsios has no science background. | Image: Getty / The Verge The Trump administration unveiled the first "Genesis Mission" grants on Thursday, directing $5 billion toward hundreds of AI-driven science projects in an effort the White House has described as "comparable in urgency and ambition to the Manhattan Project." At roughly the same time, Trump's science adviser Michael Kratsios was on Capitol Hill selling lawmakers on an equally grandiose promise: " A New Golden Age " of American science, one that would prioritize artificial intelligence, robotics, and nuclear energy and downplay life sciences. Taken together, the two announcements offer the clearest picture yet of how Donald Trump wants to remak … Read the full story at The Verge. ]]> Victoria Song https://www.theverge.com/?p=970056 2026-07-23T12:37:51-04:00 2026-07-24T10:00:00-04:00 It’s a lot of stuff, generally. | Photo: Victoria Song / The Verge This is Optimizer , a weekly newsletter sent from Verge senior
